focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 17

The flat hadn't changed.

Three days back in London, Hermione could cross it in the dark without surprise: six steps to the kitchen, four to the bathroom, radiator knocking in the same place every time. The predictability she'd chosen after the divorce should have felt like safety. Instead it felt sealed.

The Mark still found her from Wiltshire, but only as blunted data. A dull ache while she brushed her teeth. A flare at four in the morning sharp enough to wake her. The pain came loose from place. It hit while she stood at the sink or lay in the dark and told her only that somewhere in Wiltshire he was hurting. She could not tell whether he was standing or sitting or folded over the library table with his head in his hand. Whatever sustained contact had been doing to the curse, leaving had stripped it away.

By the third night sleep had reduced itself to one usable conclusion. Draco had not been angry that the house might be amplifying them. He had been angry that she had known the terms might be false and let him go on inside them anyway.

---

Thursday morning the Floo failed outright.

The Floo skipped the warning signs. At seven forty-three Hermione stepped into her fireplace, threw the powder, said, "Ministry of Magic," and got back an ordinary orange burn, fit for heat and useless for travel. Two more tries left soot on the knees of her trousers and a dark streak across one cuff.

Apparition took her to an Atrium already sliding toward panic. Technicians crowded the grates. Voices bounced off tile in that clipped professional register people used when fear would only slow them down. The outage had spread across London, parts of Bristol, most of the West Country.

Dennis Creevey intercepted her near the lifts with a stack of incident reports hugged...
open_hooks_to_carry: Draco in the archive alone — the room holds briefly, unstable. He finds the bonding texts
The realization: she has known. Her withdrawal, her careful return, the kiss — all under knowledge she hoarded
The confrontation: his anger specific to the information asymmetry, not the bond itself. She stayed. She kissed him. She treated their connection as something to study and verify
Her defense real but insufficient. His deeper wound: even this was done to him. Her deeper wound: being accused of calculation when she was drowning

payoffs_to_land.description: The secret Hermione kept since Ch11 is discovered. The specific betrayal: she continued proximity and initiated intimacy while concealing knowledge that put their entire connection in doubt

prev_tail_excerpt:
...e but not disappeared. One floor above, he was still awake. She could not hear thought through it, never that. Only the uneven pace of his heart and the shock still moving through him hard enough to echo in her and keep sleep away.

She sat on the bed and pulled her jumper over her head. Under the glamour her left forearm still held the scar, letters carved there seven years ago by a dead woman in the room downstairs. Three weeks earlier his thumb had traced that word. Tonight she could still feel the place his mouth had been, his hand at her jaw, the pull in her own body when he leaned in.

Ordinarily the research breakthrough would have kept her upright until dawn. Plaiting changed everything. Repair methodology, anchoring points, the Ministry's entire approach, all of it looked different now. Tonight the notes stayed closed on her bedside table.

Instead she lay back with her fingers pressed to her mouth and replayed the kiss: the wrong angle of his mouth, the shake in his hand before it found her jaw, the way he had turned into her palm once he stopped trying to hold himself apart. The Manor could warm a room and shorten a corridor. It could not make his hand shake.

Tomorrow she could put the folio on the desk between them and tell him the rest. She pictured his face closing before he spoke, the openness from tonight sealing back over. She would not say it.

All around her the Manor held the room at the warmest point it had yet offered her. Lavender hung in the sheets like an argument. The room was too ready for her. She didn't trust a second of it. One floor above, he was still awake. Only one storey of stone separated them. The rest was what she'd read in the folio and still wasn't telling him.

She lay there wanting him and mistrusting every part of the wanting.
state_deltas_required: Draco finds the bonding texts independently and realizes Hermione has known for weeks — the information asymmetry becomes the specific blade of betrayal
The relationship breaks. Both retreat to oldest defenses. The betrayal is not the bond — it is the secret she kept about the bond
